---
title: "hollys"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-26"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
orig <- read_csv("newdata.csv")
orig %>% filter(type=="study") %>% group_by(subj) %>% summarise(N = n(), RT=mean(RT)) -> orig_avg
recovered <- read_csv("slimstampen_model_comparison_summary.csv")
names(recovered)[1] <- "subj"
data <- orig_avg %>% left_join(recovered, by = c("subj"))
```


```{r}
variables <- c("actr_c", 
               #"actr_phi", 
               "actr_t0", 
               "actr_F", 
               "fear_d", 
               #"fear_w1", 
               "fear_t0", 
               "fear_F")
library(corrplot)
library(Hmisc)
R <-  data[variables] %>% as.matrix() %>% rcorr(type="pearson") 

corrplot(R$r, method = "circle",  diag = F, 
         type = "full", tl.col = "black", tl.srt = 45, number.cex = 0.5,
         p.mat = R$P, sig.level = 0.05, insig = "pch", pch=".", tl.cex=1,
         col=colorRampPalette(c("royalblue4", "ivory1", "firebrick4"))(200),
         cl.align.text = "l", cl.cex=1,
         addgrid.col="white",
         title = "Correlation Between Parameters",
         mar=c(0,0,1,0))


```
    

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r, fig.width=10, fig.height=10}
pred <- read_csv("slimstampen_trial_predictions.csv")%>% mutate(RT = RT/1000)

pred %>% pivot_longer(cols = c("actr_pred_RT", "fear_pred_RT"), names_to = "model", values_to = "predicted_RT") %>%
  filter(RT < 6) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=RT, y=predicted_RT, col=model)) +
  scale_color_aaas() +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5, size=0.1) +
  facet_wrap(~subj, scales="free") +
  stat_smooth(method="lm", se=T, linewidth=0.5, linetype="dashed") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position ="bottom") +
  labs(title="Predicted vs Actual RT",
       x="Actual RT",
       y="Predicted RT")
```

```{r}
pred %>% pivot_longer(cols = c("actr_pred_RT", "fear_pred_RT"), names_to = "model", values_to = "predicted_RT") %>%
group_by(subj, model) %>%
  summarise(cor = cor(RT, predicted_RT)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=model, y=cor, fill=model)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha=0.5) +
  ggsignif::geom_signif(comparisons = list(c("actr_pred_RT", "fear_pred_RT")), 
                     test = "t.test", 
                     map_signif_level = TRUE, 
                     tip_length = 0.01) +
  scale_fill_aaas() +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position ="bottom") +
  labs(title="Correlation of Predicted vs Actual RT by Model",
       x="Model",
       y="Correlation (r)")
```

```{r}
pred %>% pivot_longer(cols = c("actr_phi", "actr_c", "actr_t0", "fear_w1", "fear_d", "fear_t0"), names_to = "parameter", values_to = "value") %>%
  group_by(parameter, subj, item) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(value)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=mean_value, fill = parameter)) +
  scale_fill_aaas() +
  geom_histogram(col="white", alpha=0.5, bins = 8) +
  facet_wrap(~parameter, scales="free") +
  theme_minimal() 

```

```{r}
pred %>% group_by(subj, item) %>%
  filter(repetition == max(repetition)) %>%
  filter(repetition > 2) %>%
  summarise(mean_actr_phi = mean(actr_phi), 
            mean_ml_phi = mean(alpha),
            mean_fear_w1 = mean(fear_w1)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c("mean_actr_phi", "mean_fear_w1"), names_to = "parameter", values_to = "mean_value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(y=mean_value, x=mean_ml_phi)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~parameter, scales="free_y") +
  stat_smooth(method="lm", se=T, linewidth=0.5, linetype="dashed") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title="Mean ACT-R phi vs Mean FEAR w1",
       x="Mean ML phi",
       y="Mean Value")
```

```{r}
pred %>% group_by(subj, item) %>%
  filter(repetition == max(repetition)) %>%
  filter(repetition > 2) %>%
  group_by(subj) %>%
  summarise(sof = mean(alpha)) %>%
  left_join(recovered, by = "subj") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=fear_d, y=sof)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5) +
  stat_smooth(method="lm", se=T, linewidth=0.5) +
  theme_minimal() 
              
```

```{r}
pred %>% group_by(subj, item) %>%
  filter(repetition == max(repetition)) %>%
  
  pivot_wider(id_cols = c(subj), names_from = item, values_from = actr_phi) %>% 
  select(-subj) %>%
  as.matrix() %>% rcorr(type="spearman") -> R

print(mean(R$r))
  
corrplot(R$r, method = "circle",  diag = F, 
         type = "full", tl.col = "black", tl.srt = 45, number.cex = 0.5,
         p.mat = R$P, sig.level = 0.05, insig = "pch", pch=".", tl.cex=1,
         col=colorRampPalette(c("royalblue4", "ivory1", "firebrick4"))(200),
         cl.align.text = "l", cl.cex=1,
         addgrid.col="white",
         title = "Correlation Between FEAR w1 Across Items",
         mar=c(0,0,1,0))
```
Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

# Fit 5
ACTR: phi, c, s by subject
FEAR: d, w1, s by subject
FEAR wins LL/AIC/BIC, LL is good in all ss, no diff in correlations, but ACTR is better

# Fit 4
ACTR: phi, c, t0 by subject
FEAR: d, w1, t0 by subject
FEAR wins LL/AIC/BIC, LL is good in all ss, no diff in correlations.but FEAR is better (0.32)

# Fit 6
ACTR: phi, c, F by subject
FEAR: d, w1, F by subject
FF wins LL/AIC/BIC (33/35), LL is good in all ss, no diff in correlations.but FEAR is better (0.34)

# Fit 7
ACTR: phi, c, tau by subject
FEAR: d, w1, tau by subject
FEAR wins LL/AIC/BIC (22/35), LL is good in all ss, ACT-R much better corr than fear (0.33 > 0.1).

# Fit 8
ACTR: phi, c, tau, F, t0 by subject
FEAR: d, w1, tau, F, t0 by subject
FEAR wins LL/AIC/BIC (23/35), LL is good in all ss, ACT-R much better corr than fear (0.33 > 0.1).

# Fit 9
ACTR: phi, c, F, t0 by subject
FEAR: d, w1, F, t0 by subject
FEAR wins LL/AIC/BIC: (27/35) subjects, LL good in all ss, FEAR slightly better corr (0.34 > 0.32)

# Fit 10
ACTR: phi, c, tau, t0 by subject
FEAR: d, w1, tau, t0 by subject
FEAR wins LL/AIC/BIC (28/35), LL good in all, no diff in correlations, ACT-R better corr (0.32 > 0.20)

# Fit 11
ACTR: phi, c, tau, F by subject
FEAR: d, w1, tau, t by subject
FEAR wins LL/AIC/BIC (26/35), LL good in most, ACT-R significant better corr (0.34 > 0.11)

-- by fact ---

ACTR: c, t0 by subject, phi by fact
FEAR: w1, t0 by subject, d by fact
ACTR wins LL/AIC/BIC

# Fit 12
ACTR: c, t0 by subject, phi by fact
FEAR: d, t0 by subject, w1 by fact
FEAR wins LL/AIC/BIC (23/35), no difference in corr (~ .5)

# Fit 15
ACTR: c, tau by subject, phi by fact
FEAR: d, tau by subject, w1 by fact
ACT-R wins LL/AIC/BIC (9/35), mean = 6.66, ACT-R sign better corr (~ .5 > 0.1)

# Fit 13
ACTR: c, F by subject, phi by fact
FEAR: d, F by subject, w1 by fact
FE-ACT-R wins AIC: 20/35 subjects, no diff in corr (~.5)


# Fit 14
ACTR: c, F, t0 by subject, phi by fact
FEAR: d, F, t0 by subject, w1 by fact
FE-ACT-R wins AIC: 24/35 subjects (-6.5), noi diff in corr (~5), ACTR slightly better 

# Fit 16
ACTR: c, F, tau by subject, phi by fact
FEAR: d, F, tau by subject, w1 by fact
ACT-R wins LL/AIC/BIC (5/35), mean = 12.66, ACT-R better corr (~ .5 > 0.1)

# Fit 17
ACTR: c, F, t0, tau by subject, phi by fact
FEAR: d, F, t0, tau by subject, w1 by fact
ACT-R wins LL/AIC/BIC (6/35), mean = 11.76, ACT-R better corr (~ .58 > 0.23)

# Fit 18
ACTR: c, t0, tau by subject, phi by fact
FEAR: d, t0, tau by subject, w1 by fact
FE-ACT-R wins AIC: 23/35 subjects, Mean ΔBIC (FE - ACT-R): -6.21

# Fit 19
ACTR: c, t0, s by subject, phi by fact
FEAR: d, t0, s by subject, w1 by fact
FE-ACT-R wins AIC: 22/35 subjects, Mean ΔBIC (FE - ACT-R): -0.81


- other

ACTR: c, s by subject; phi by fact
FEAR: d, s by subject; w1 by fact
ACTR wins LL/AIC/BIC, LL is good in all ss, ACT-R RT correlations are better

ACTR: c by subject; phi by fact
FEAR: d by subject; w1 by fact
ACTR wins LL/AIC/BIC, LL is good in all ss, ACT-R RT correlations are better

ACTR: c, t0 by subject; phi by fact
FEAR: d, t0 by subject; w1 by fact
FEAR wins LL/AIC/BIC, LL is good in all ss, no diff correlations are better

ACTR: c, by subject; phi, t0 by fact
FEAR: d, y subject; w1, t0 by fact
FEAR wins LL/AIC/BIC, LL is good in all ss, no diff correlations 

ACTR: c constant 0.5, t0 by fact; phi by subject
FEAR: w1 constant 2.6, t0 by fact; w1 by subject
FEAR barely wins LL/AIC/BIC, LL is good in all ss, no diff correlations ACT-R better

ACTR: c  0.5, phi by fact
FEAR: d by subject, w1 by fact
FEAR barely wins LL/AIC/BIC, LL is good in all ss, no diff correlations ACT-R better
